frameworks.name: attentional blink
frameworks.one_line: T2 identification suffers when following T1 within ~900 ms in RSVP

frameworks.name: limited-resource attention bottleneck
frameworks.one_line: T1 and T2 compete for limited processing capacity, predicting larger blink when T1 is harder

core_question: Does increasing T1 perceptual difficulty (via contrast or exposure duration) modulate the magnitude of the attentional blink on T2?
key_assumptions: T1 and T2 share limited processing resources
Prolonged T1 processing time should enlarge the blink
Perceptual difficulty can be operationalized via contrast or exposure duration
manipulations: T1 exposure duration (10 ms hard vs longer easy)
T1 contrast (individually titrated to match identification accuracy)
key_findings: Robust attentional blink replicated across all conditions
No difference in blink magnitude between hard, long-duration, and high-contrast T1 conditions
interpretations: Increasing T1 perceptual difficulty via contrast or duration is not sufficient to modulate the attentional blink
